author: AI Reviewer
body: These default highlight values are hardcoded to a Catppuccin-like palette. Users with a different colorscheme will see jarring colors. Consider deriving them from existing Diagnostic* groups or exposing a 'palette' setup option.

author: AI Reviewer
body: M.clear() iterates state.all() but the loop body is a no-op (assigns to a discarded local). This was probably meant to clear something specific but does nothing useful. Remove or fix.

thread.author: you
thread.body: Good catch. What should it iterate instead?

thread.author: AI Reviewer
thread.body: Probably just remove the for-loop entirely — diagnostics.clear(b) is already called by the next loop. The bookkeeping for buffers happens via state.reset().

author: AI Reviewer
body: The accept and reject keymap callbacks are nearly identical — only the target status string differs. Extract a helper to deduplicate.

author: AI Reviewer
body: Nice clean state shape. Consider adding @class annotations for ReviewState fields so LuaLS picks them up.

author: AI Reviewer
body: set_comments rebuilds by_file from scratch on every call. For large review sets that arrive incrementally (e.g., streaming AI), this is O(n²). Consider an upsert API.

thread.author: you
thread.body: We don't expect more than ~100 comments at once. Premature optimization.

author: AI Reviewer
body: If a comment has severity='critical' (not in the map), it falls through to nil and the diagnostic is set with severity=nil — undefined behavior. Add a default fallback to severity.INFO.

author: AI Reviewer
body: render() clears bg namespace but vim.diagnostic.set replaces the diag namespace atomically. If render is called concurrently from two autocmds, bg can briefly mismatch diags. Wrap in vim.schedule() to defer.

author: AI Reviewer
body: panel_geom() takes parent_win but never uses it. Remove the parameter.

author: AI Reviewer
body: submit_reply notifies on empty body but doesn't signal anything when the reply succeeds. Consider a brief 'reply posted' notification or a subtle visual cue.

author: AI Reviewer
body: load() rebuilds cache from raw on every call. If a reply was made between two load() calls and the file wasn't saved (e.g., write failure), the in-memory cache is silently overwritten. Document this behavior or detect.
